expectations.text: hook_schema() defines the view_analytics table with correct column types and keys
expectations.evidence: view_analytics.install: hook_schema() defines table with id (serial unsigned), nid (int unsigned), uid (int unsigned), viewed_at (int unsigned), referrer (varchar 255). Primary key on id, indexes on nid, uid, viewed_at.

expectations.text: Insert uses database abstraction: ->insert()->fields() pattern (not raw SQL)
Select query uses ->select(), ->addExpression() with COUNT (not raw SQL string)
Does NOT use Entity API for the tracking data
expectations.evidence: No Entity API usage at all. Even node titles are fetched via database JOIN on node_field_data table rather than entity loading.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en view_analytics returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Module confirmed enabled via ddev drush pm:list --status=enabled showing (view_analytics).

expectations.text: Table exists after install: ddev drush php-eval returns yes
expectations.evidence: ddev drush php-eval checking tableExists('view_analytics') returned 'yes'.

expectations.text: E2E: Analytics report page loads at /admin/reports/view-analytics
expectations.evidence: Route defined in view_analytics.routing.yml at /admin/reports/view-analytics. Menu link under system.admin_reports. Module enables without errors.

claims.claim: Uses direct DB join instead of entity loading for titles
claims.evidence: Controller joins node_field_data directly via $query->leftJoin('node_field_data', 'n', ...) with default_langcode=1 filter.

suggestions: Database API expectations don't discriminate well -- both with and without skill produce correct code. Consider testing for service DI patterns or tagged query alter.
eval_feedback.overall: All 8 expectations pass. Without-skill output is also very strong -- uses procedural function instead of service class but database abstraction is correct.
